GitHub - tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter: Go bindings for tree-sitter

github.com/tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter
This repository contains Go bindings for the Tree-sitter parsing library. To use this in your Go project, run: By default, none of the grammars are included in this package. This way, you can only bring in what you need, but it's at the slight cost of having to call go get n times. Alternatively you ...

GitHub - smacker/go-tree-sitter: Golang bindings for tree-sitter https ...

github.com/smacker/go-tree-sitter
This repository provides grammars for many common languages out of the box. But if you need support for any other language you can keep it inside your own project or publish it as a separate repository to share with the community. See explanation on how to create a grammar for go-tree-sitter here. Known external grammars: Salesforce grammars - including Apex, SOQL, and SOSL languages. Ruby ...

github.com/tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter - Go Packages

pkg.go.dev/github.com/tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter     2025-02-02T00:00:00.0000000
README ¶ Go Tree-sitter This repository contains Go bindings for the Tree-sitter parsing library. To use this in your Go project, run: ... Example usage: ... By default, none of the grammars are included in this package. This way, you can only bring in what you need, but it's at the slight cost of having to call go get n times.

Tinkering with Tree-Sitter Using Go - DEV Community

dev.to/shrsv/tinkering-with-tree-sitter-using-go-4d8n     2025-05-07T00:00:00.0000000
This journey into Tree-Sitter with Go has been a fun way to understand code parsing. The examples here are just the start—play with different snippets, explore the AST, and build something cool. If you hit snags, the Tree-Sitter community and docs are great resources. Happy coding! *AI agents write code fast.

tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter | DeepWiki

deepwiki.com/tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter     2025-04-27T00:00:00.0000000
Tree-sitter is a parser generator tool and incremental parsing library that can build a concrete syntax tree for source code and efficiently update the syntax tree as the source code is edited. The go-tree-sitter repository provides Go language bindings to this C library, allowing Go applications to use Tree-sitter's parsing capabilities.

Installation and Setup | tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter | DeepWiki

deepwiki.com/tree-sitter/go-tree-sitter/1.1-installation-and-setup     2025-04-27T00:00:00.0000000
Installation and Setup Relevant source files This page provides instructions for installing the Tree-sitter Go bindings and setting up a project to use them. For a high-level overview of the system architecture, see Overview. Prerequisites Before installing go-tree-sitter, ensure you have: Go 1.23 or later (as specified in the module requirements) A C compiler (required for CGO compilation ...

libraries.io/go/github.com/smacker/go-tree-sitter
See explanation on how to create a grammar for go-tree-sitter here. Known external grammars: Salesforce grammars - including Apex, SOQL, and SOSL languages. Ruby - Deprecated, grammar is provided by main repo instead Go Template - Used for helm

Digging Deeper into Code with Tree-Sitter: How to Query Your Syntax ...

dev.to/shailendra53/digging-deeper-into-code-with-tree-sitter-how-to-query-your-syntax-tree-3i1     2025-05-14T00:00:00.0000000
This is another very interesting feature of Tree-sitter that can help you get deeper insights into your source code. For example, you can write a query to list all the functions present in a repo's source code. And that's exactly what we'll explore in this post. Just like the last post, I'll use GoLang for demonstrating the use cases.

Introduction - Tree-sitter

tree-sitter.github.io/tree-sitter/
Tree-sitter Introduction Tree-sitter is a parser generator tool and an incremental parsing library. It can build a concrete syntax tree for a source file and efficiently update the syntax tree as the source file is edited. Tree-sitter aims to be: General enough to parse any programming language Fast enough to parse on every keystroke in a text ...
